title: Comparison & Best Practices
objectives: Compare all approaches on key axes
Pick the right tool for a given app

sections.markdown: There is no universally 'best' state manager — only the best fit for your app size, team, and complexity. The goal is the same everywhere: separate UI from logic, rebuild granularly, and keep state testable.

A great answer to 'which state management?' is: 'It depends — setState for local UI, Riverpod or BLoC for most production apps (testable, scalable), and I avoid one-size-fits-all. I separate logic from UI and rebuild granularly regardless of the tool.'

Universal best practices: keep business logic out of widgets; make state immutable; rebuild only what changed (Selector/select/Consumer); model async as loading/error/data; and make state unit-testable without the widget tree.
You can mix approaches: setState for trivial local state and Riverpod/BLoC for shared domain state in the same app. Consistency within a feature matters more than picking one global winner.

flashcards.back: Riverpod, BLoC/Cubit, and Redux — logic is separated and easily unit-tested.

flashcards.front: Best default for most production apps?
flashcards.back: Riverpod or BLoC — scalable, testable, with clear UI/logic separation.
Separate logic from UI, immutable state, granular rebuilds, testable logic.

quiz.explain: Fit beats dogma; there's no universal winner.

quiz.q: Which is best for quick prototypes?

quiz.explain: Granular rebuilds and immutability apply to every approach.

challenges.text: List which approach you'd pick for 3 app scenarios and why.
Build the same todo feature in Provider and Riverpod; compare.
Implement one feature in BLoC and Redux; compare boilerplate and testability.
Write a decision guide for your team mapping app traits to a recommended approach.

interviewQuestions: Which state management do you prefer and why?
How do you choose a state approach for a new app?
What best practices apply to ALL approaches?
Can you mix multiple approaches in one app?
